conjecture_title: Open problem: p-entropic matroids linear for prime p
summary: The conjecture that p-entropic matroids are F_p-representable (linear) for every prime p remains open as of 2026. The source paper proved this for p=2 and p=3, and ruled out linearity for p=9 (a composite), but no follow-up work resolving the question for larger primes (p=5, 7, 11, ...) was found in the literature. The related 2022 paper by Kühne and Yashfe (arXiv:2206.03465) on entropic and almost-multilinear representability of matroids touches adjacent territory but does not appear to settle this specific conjecture.
search_queries: entropic matroids linear prime representation Abbe Spirkl
"entropic matroids" prime "p=5" OR "p=7" OR "p=11" linear representable 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: No follow-up found that resolves the conjecture for primes p > 3. The paper arXiv:2206.03465 (Kühne–Yashfe, 2022) is titled 'On entropic and almost multilinear representability of matroids' and is the closest related post-2019 work found, but WebFetch of its abstract did not confirm it addresses the p-entropic/prime conjecture specifically. Confidence is medium rather than high because the conjecture is ~6 years old and the searches, while covering the main venues, may not have caught all relevant work.
